title: Rustic Media Console Cabinet
summary: A 1x12 media console with a big lower drawer, an adjustable center shelf, and built-up legs. Beadboard back and a cut-out footer give it the rustic look.
description:
This is a media console built almost entirely from 1x12 pine — a straightforward box with a deep lower drawer, an adjustable shelf above it for components, and legs built up from 1x2 and 1x3 wrapped around the corners. It's all butt joints, glue, and brad nails, so it's an accessible build, but the proportions and the details (the beadboard back, the scroll-cut footer) are what make it read as furniture rather than a crate.

The legs are the structural trick worth understanding. Instead of turned or solid legs, you wrap 1x2 sides and 1x3 fronts/backs around each corner and nail them into the shelves, the sides, and each other. That wrap is what actually ties the cabinet together — the note in the plan is explicit that nailing the leg fronts into the shelves 'will keep the cabinet together.' So don't treat the legs as decoration you add last; they're doing real work.

The footer is where you get to play: cut a decorative profile into the 1x4 footer pieces with a jigsaw, use the first one as a template for the rest, and you've got a custom look for the price of a few saw cuts. The center shelf sits on adjustable shelf-pin brackets so you can set component height after the fact. Finish it rustic — a wiping stain or a distressed paint suits the style better than a glassy clear coat.

tools.note: For setting nails if you're hand-nailing.

materials.name: 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Top trim, leg sides, shelf trim.

steps.title: Join the sides to the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the two cabinet sides to the bottom shelf with glue and 2" nails, driving through the sides into the end edges of the shelf. Line up the bottom edges flush. This sets the width of the whole cabinet, so get it square from the start.

steps.title: Add the drawer guides and middle shelf
steps.body: Attach the two drawer guides with 1-1/4" nails and glue — they need to sit 1" wide by 3/4" tall to carry the drawer. Then add the middle shelf 7-1/4" up from the bottom of the cabinet (measured to the bottom of the shelf), using 2" nails and glue.

steps.title: Cap the top and check for square
steps.body: Nail the cabinet top in place, driving through the top into the top edges of the sides with glue and 2" nails. This is the moment to check the whole box for square — measure the diagonals and rack it into line before anything else goes on, because every trim piece from here references these edges.

steps.title: Add the top trim
steps.body: Attach the front and back top trim pieces with glue and 2" nails, keeping the top and outside edges flush, securing into both the cabinet top and the side trim pieces. This frames the top and hides the end grain of the sides.

steps.title: Build up the legs
steps.body: Add the 1x2 leg sides first, keeping front and back edges flush, with 1-1/4" nails and glue. Then add the 1x3 leg fronts and backs, outside edges flush with the sides, and nail the fronts into the shelves, the sides, and the leg sides. This wrap is what actually locks the cabinet together — it's structural, not just trim.

steps.title: Cut and attach the decorative footer
steps.body: Cut a decorative profile into the footer pieces with a jigsaw — use your own design, then use the first cut piece as a template for the others so they match. Nail the footer pieces in place. This is the detail that makes the console look intentional rather than boxy.

steps.title: Add the back, shelf trim, drawer, and finish
steps.body:
Attach the beadboard back panel, then the front middle shelf trim with its top edge flush to the middle shelf. Build the drawer from the 1x6 and 1x12 parts and fit it on the guides. Add the adjustable shelf on shelf-pin brackets so you can set component height later.

Fill nail holes, sand, and finish rustic — a wiping stain or a distressed paint suits this design better than a glossy clear coat.

images.alt: Rustic wooden media console cabinet with a lower drawer and beadboard back
